SEARCH
Package be.ac.umons.backend.pst

Class PST

java.lang.Object
be.ac.umons.backend.pst.PST
Direct Known Subclasses:
PstH, PstV
public abstract class PST extends Object
Abstract Priority Search Tree. Stores a list of segments and supports range queries of the form (-inf : heapMax] x [sortMin : sortMax] in O(log n + k). Subclasses define which coordinate plays the role of the heap axis and which plays the role of the sort axis, allowing the same structure to handle both horizontal and vertical segments without coordinate inversion. Construction is done in O(n log n) via a single sort before recursion. Each recursive call then costs O(n) only.

Convention : sortCoord <= yMid goes left, sortCoord > yMid goes right.

  • Field Details

    • root

      private PstNode root
      The root node of the PST.

  • Constructor Details

    • PST

      public PST()

  • Method Details

    • getHeapCoord

      protected abstract double getHeapCoord(Segment s)
      Returns the heap coordinate of the stored endpoint of a segment. Used for the heap property (minimum at root). x for horizontal segments, y for vertical segments.
      Parameters:
      s - the segment
      Returns:
      heap coordinate of the stored endpoint

    • getSortCoord

      protected abstract double getSortCoord(Segment s)
      Returns the sort coordinate of the stored endpoint of a segment. Used for sorting and splitting into subtrees. y for horizontal segments, x for vertical segments.
      Parameters:
      s - the segment
      Returns:
      sort coordinate of the stored endpoint

    • getOtherHeapCoord

      protected abstract double getOtherHeapCoord(Segment s)
      Returns the heap coordinate of the other endpoint of a segment. Used to verify the heapMin bound during queries. x for horizontal segments, y for vertical segments.
      Parameters:
      s - the segment
      Returns:
      heap coordinate of the other endpoint

    • build

      public void build(List<Segment> segments)
      Builds the PST from a list of segments in O(n log n). Sorts by sort coordinate first (composite numbers if equal), then builds recursively. Each recursive call costs O(n).
      Parameters:
      segments - list of segments to store

    • buildRecursive

      private PstNode buildRecursive(List<Segment> segments)
      Recursive construction on a list already sorted by sort coordinate. At each call : find heap minimum in O(n), read median in O(1), split by index in O(1). Total cost : O(n log n).
      Parameters:
      segments - sorted list of segments
      Returns:
      root node of the constructed subtree

    • query

      public List<Segment> query(double heapMin, double heapMax, double sortMin, double sortMax)
      Queries the PST with a range (-inf : heapMax] x [sortMin : sortMax]. heapMin is verified via the other endpoint reference. Returns all candidate segments in O(log n + k). For PstH : heapMin=xMin, heapMax=xMax, sortMin=yMin, sortMax=yMax. For PstV : heapMin=yMin, heapMax=yMax, sortMin=xMin, sortMax=xMax.
      Parameters:
      heapMin - lower bound on the heap coordinate (checked via other endpoint)
      heapMax - upper bound on the heap coordinate
      sortMin - lower bound on the sort coordinate
      sortMax - upper bound on the sort coordinate
      Returns:
      list of candidate segments

    • queryPst

      private void queryPst(PstNode root, double heapMin, double heapMax, double sortMin, double sortMax, List<Segment> res)
      Three phases : 1. Descend to vSplit, the node where paths to sortMin and sortMax diverge. 2. Descend toward sortMin in the left subtree of vSplit : when the path goes left, report the entire right subtree. 3. Descend toward sortMax in the right subtree of vSplit : when the path goes right, report the entire left subtree.
      Parameters:
      root - current node
      heapMin - lower bound on heap coordinate
      heapMax - upper bound on heap coordinate
      sortMin - lower bound on sort coordinate
      sortMax - upper bound on sort coordinate
      res - list to add results to

    • reportInSubTree

      private void reportInSubTree(PstNode root, double heapMin, double heapMax, List<Segment> res)
      Reports all segments in the subtree whose heap coordinate is at most heapMax.d heapMin is verified via the other endpoint.
      Parameters:
      root - root of the subtree
      heapMin - lower bound on heap coordinate (checked via other endpoint)
      heapMax - upper bound on heap coordinate
      res - list to add results to

    • testAndReport

      private void testAndReport(PstNode root, double heapMin, double heapMax, double sortMin, double sortMax, List<Segment> res)
      Tests whether the segment stored at a node lies in the query range and reports it if so.
      Parameters:
      root - node to test
      heapMin - lower bound on heap coordinate (checked via other endpoint)
      heapMax - upper bound on heap coordinate
      sortMin - lower bound on sort coordinate
      sortMax - upper bound on sort coordinate
      res - list to add result to
